description: Core combination vaccine protecting against four serious canine diseases. Distemper is often fatal; Parvovirus is highly contagious and deadly in puppies.
booster_freq: Every 1-3 years depending on vaccine type and local laws

description: Core vaccine required by law in all US states. Fatal zoonotic disease transmissible to humans.
booster_freq: Every 1-3 years depending on state law and vaccine type

description: Non-core vaccine protecting against the most common bacterial cause of infectious tracheobronchitis. Recommended for dogs that board, attend daycare, groomers, or dog parks.
interval_doses: None (single dose); some brands require 2-dose initial

description: Non-core vaccine against a bacterial disease spread through wildlife urine in water/soil. Can cause kidney/liver failure and is zoonotic. Increasingly recommended as disease spreads.
Non-core vaccine for dogs in tick-endemic regions (Northeast, Upper Midwest, Pacific Coast). Prevents the most common tick-borne disease.
Non-core vaccine for dogs in high-risk environments (kennels, shows, urban areas with outbreaks). Two strains; bivalent vaccine covers both.
Core combination vaccine. Panleukopenia (feline distemper) is frequently fatal; herpesvirus and calicivirus cause severe upper respiratory disease.
booster_freq: Every 1-3 years depending on vaccine type and risk

description: Core vaccine required by law in most jurisdictions. Fatal zoonotic disease. Even indoor cats should be vaccinated.
booster_freq: Every 1-3 years depending on vaccine type and local law

description: Non-core vaccine recommended for all kittens (due to uncertain future lifestyle) and outdoor/indoor-outdoor adult cats. FeLV is a leading cause of cancer and immunosuppression in cats.
